title: Visitor Log Completeness for Data Center Escorted Access

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 9.4.4 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 9.4.4 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.57: Visitor Log Completeness for Data Center Escorted Access.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.57' running on index=physical visitor:checkin; pacs:door escort reader pairings, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=physical sourcetype="visitor:checkin" earliest=-7d
| eval visit_key=md5(concat(visitor_id,date_hour,site))
| join type=left max=0 visit_key [
    search index=physical sourcetype="pacs:door" earliest=-7d escort_flag="true"
    | eval visit_key=md5(concat(visitor_id,date_hour,site))
    | stats count as escort_swipes by visit_key
  ]
| where isnull(escort_swipes) OR escort_swipes=0
| table _time, visitor_id, site, host_escort_expected
| sort _time
description: Compares visitor check-in events from front desk systems to escorted badge-in events inside the CDE suite so visitor management procedures are complete and auditable.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes physical and sourcetypes pacs:door, visitor:checkin. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Align `visitor_id` keys between systems. (2) Define mandatory escort readers. (3) Daily exception report to facilities. (4) Store signed visitor logs for assessor samples. Indexes required: `index=physical`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pacs:door`, `sourcetype=visitor:checkin`.
visualization: Table (missing escorts), single value (open exceptions), column chart (by site).
knownFalsePositives: CDE access from authorized payment ops, scheduled batch reconciliation, or PCI assessor activities.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect or `sourcetype="visitor:checkin"` HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=physical` `visitor:checkin`; `pacs:door` escort reader pairings.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align `visitor_id` keys between systems. (2) Define mandatory escort readers. (3) Daily exception report to facilities. (4) Store signed visitor logs for assessor samples.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="visitor:checkin" earliest=-7d
| eval visit_key=md5(concat(visitor_id,date_hour,site))
| join type=left max=0 visit_key [
    search index=physical sourcetype="pacs:door" earliest=-7d escort_flag="true"
    | eval visit_key=md5(concat(visitor_id,date_hour,site))
    | stats count as escort_swipes by visit_key
  ]
| where isnull(escort_swipes) OR escort_swipes=0
| table _time, visitor_id, site, host_escort_expected
| sort _time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Visitor Log Completeness for Data Center Escorted Access (PCI DSS Req 9.4.1, 9.4.4)** — Compares visitor check-in events from front desk systems to escorted badge-in events inside the CDE suite so visitor management procedures are complete and auditable.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=physical` `visitor:checkin`; `pacs:door` escort reader pairings. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect or `sourcetype="visitor:checkin"` HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: physical; **sourcetype**: visitor:checkin. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=physical, sourcetype="visitor:checkin", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **visit_key** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(escort_swipes) OR escort_swipes=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: _time, escort_swipes, host_escort_expected, site, visit_key, visitor_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (missing escorts), single value (open exceptions), column chart (by site).
grandmaExplanation: We compare visitor check-in events from front desk systems to escorted badge-in events inside the CDE suite so visitor management procedures are complete and auditable. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Authentication (CIM data model).
